title: transform_request_with_thinking ignores reasoning_effort for non-reasoning models silently
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/providers/openai.rs:39-66
## Violation
When thinking.enabled is true but model is not o1/o3/o4, the code silently drops the configured reasoning_effort with only a debug! log. A user who configures effort=high for gpt-4 gets no warning that their config is ineffective. Consider that the proxy operator can configure ThinkingConfig.enabled=true globally and select the model per request — a misconfigured request returns success without telling the operator that thinking did not actually run.
## Mandated refactor
Emit a warn! log when thinking.enabled is true but model is unsupported. Better: surface as a typed warning in the response so the caller can detect (e.g., add openclaudia.thinking_skipped: bool to response metadata). Best: validate at request construction time and return ProviderError::Unsupported when the combination is incompatible with explicit effort.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
